turns.user_message: I used to wake up with this quiet excitement about tackling the day, but lately I just feel a heavy drag in my chest. I force myself to sit down and get started, only to stare blankly while the hours slip away. It is frustrating because I know I am capable of pushing through, yet my brain feels completely foggy. I keep hoping tomorrow will bring back that old spark, but right now I just feel stuck in a slow, exhausting loop.

I remember when I could power through long stretches without losing my focus, but now even simple routines drain me completely by midday. I try to push forward like I always used to, but my patience snaps at every minor interruption. The daily grind feels heavier every single week, and I catch myself dreading the morning alarm more and more. I keep telling myself it is just a rough patch, but the constant mental fatigue is wearing me down to the bone.

I used to thrive under pressure and actually enjoy the challenge, but now the sheer weight of everything makes me want to just step away. I sit there trying to force myself to move, yet my mind completely blanks out whenever I look at what needs to be done. The disconnect between who I was and who I am right now is honestly terrifying to sit with. I feel completely hollowed out, running on fumes while watching my own drive crumble into dust.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be.
